children.heading: Block grant program
children.label: a Block grant program
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall make grants to eligible States to enable such States to carry out the activities described in subsection (e).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, a State, acting on its own behalf or on behalf of a person, shall submit to the Secretary an application that contains such information and is in such form as may be required by the Secretary.

children.heading: Demonstration of need
children.label: 2 Demonstration of need
children.text: In the application submitted under paragraph (1), the State shall demonstrate a need for the activities described in subsection (e) and provide a description of those activities and projects that will receive financial assistance from a grant made under this section to the State.

children.heading: Minimum amount
children.label: 1 Minimum amount
children.text: Each State that submits for a fiscal year an application under subsection (b) that meets the requirements of the Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, receive a grant in an amount determined in accordance with paragraph (3).

children.heading: Programs of national significance
children.label: 2 Programs of national significance
children.text: Of amounts appropriated or otherwise available to carry out this section for any fiscal year, the Secretary shall reserve 5 percent to be provided for activities and projects of national significance or projects expected to have a significant impact in preventing the abuse of drugs by youth.

children.label: I
children.text: 25 percent equally among the eligible States if such amount is less than $40,000,000; or

children.label: II
children.text: $250,000 to each eligible State if such amount equals or exceeds $40,000,000;

children.label: ii
children.text: allot one-half of 1 percent of such amount on the basis of need among Guam, American Samoa, the Virgin Islands of the United States, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands; and

children.label: iii
children.text: set aside the remainder to be disbursed as described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: the highest proportions of school-aged youth are at risk of drug abuse;1So in original. Probably should be “who are”.1

children.label: ii
children.text: if a tangible need has been identified by the State involved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the State involved has proposed the funding of additional projects targeted at the areas of highest need;
projects aimed at youth who are not in school or who are at risk of dropping out of school;

projects that seek to reinvolve dropouts in educational programs, involve youth community-based activities, develop training or employment opportunities for dropouts, or provide youth with alternatives to drug abuse;
projects to provide after-school, vacation, and weekend activities designed to give youth opportunities to actively participate in a variety of activities, including youth sports programs;
activities and projects that are consistent with activities and projects described in subsection (e)(1) and that include participation by the business community;
projects that provide outreach to individuals of all ages who are at high risk of involvement with drug abuse;

projects targeted to communities with the most serious drug abuse problems to enable such communities to develop programs that coordinate Federal, State, and local efforts to develop comprehensive, long-term, community-wide prevention and education strategies;

children.label: A
children.text: educational programs;

children.label: B
children.text: community-based activities;

children.label: C
children.text: training or employment opportunities; or

children.label: D
children.text: other alternatives to gang involvement;

children.label: A
children.text: education and instruction consistent with part A of title IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 7101

children.label: B
children.text: athletic activities;

children.label: C
children.text: creative activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: other programs designed to reduce the risk of drug abuse; and
projects that seek to inform youth regarding the existence and operation of the projects referred to in paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: local educational agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: law enforcement agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: community-based organizations;

children.label: D
children.text: community action agencies;

children.label: E
children.text: local or State recreational departments; or

children.label: F
children.text: business organizations; and

children.heading: Other activities and projects
children.label: 2 Other activities and projects
children.text: Such assistance may be provided to carry out projects or activities that are consistent with the activities and projects described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Project evaluations
children.label: f Project evaluations
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the evaluation of activities and projects conducted with financial assistance received under this section. Applications for grants under this section shall include a description of the method to be used in evaluating the impact such activities and programs have on the drug abuse problem within the communities in which such activities and projects are carried out.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $40,000,000 for fiscal year 1989, $55,000,000 for fiscal year 1990, $60,000,000 for fiscal year 1991, $66,550,000 for fiscal year 1992, and $73,205,000 for fiscal year 1993.
